author: @JehuMcSpooran
text: This would be awesome for diabetics. Imagine an implant that is a constant blood glucose monitor that is powered by the glucose in the blood. Heck, it could be a step towards a true artificial pancreas. Sure, it might not be able to make insulin but it could burn excess glucose in the blood. It could be handy if ethanol powered too. Could you imagine a pacemaker that required you to be constantly drunk?

author: @AtlasReburdened
text:
Have you read about the MIT team that's polished up their(I think they were the shock electrodialysis team)water desalination/purification work and produced a 2nd or 3rd order prototype with incredible specs? Something like 7 liters of potable water per day at a cost of something tiny like 120wh and with straight sea water as the source. 
If the numbers I saw were right and I'm not terribly mistaken, it's tech that could quickly change the world.

author: @michaelschauperl172
text:
Hi Robert 
I ve got a question you might be able to answer

It's about the cooling effect of Zeolithe x13 and how rapitly it takes place.

I do have one of those Gosun Solar Cookers With a Vacuum glass tubes. It can heat stuff up to about 250 C°. It even works in winter. I do cook with it quite often but as i don't cook all the time , it just sits in the shelf waiting to be used most oft the time. So I thought why not heating zeolithe x13 with it.

But the vacuum-tube is made from glass and the manufacturer says the metal tray that goes inside  shouln't be loaded with frozen stuff as the heat difference could coud cause thermal shock 
and I don't want to break the tube.

So do you think what would happen if i filled the metal tray with zeolithe x13. 
Will it cool down that much?

Or is there safe a way to test it.
I want to build a small  oven -  that can be filled with the granulate and water.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: doesn't X13 get hot the with addition of water mate ?

replies.author: @michaelschauperl172
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  oh... i ve written it wrong. I wan't to heat the Zeolithe in the vacuum tube. And later use it in a small oven...  where I can put zeolithe and water in.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: @michaelschauperl172 ah ok - it makes sense to me now mate - thanks for clarifying - it should be fine - it will basically boil the water away but your 'wet' zeolite should be around ambient when you put it in - you can do that just by letting the zeolite water mix sit around for a bit

replies.author: @michaelschauperl172
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering Thank you very much. I ll show you the result.
